content: Answer in one sentence and do not repeat any phrase: what is a tree?
A tree is a plant with a trunk, branches, and leaves.
If your answer is complete, stop after one sentence: what is a file path?
A file path tells you where a file is located on a computer.
Answer briefly and stop when complete: what is a checkpoint?
A checkpoint is a saved training state used for resume or evaluation.
Explain what a tokenizer does in two short sentences and do not repeat yourself.
A tokenizer splits text into token pieces. The model reads those token ids instead of raw text.
Explain what inference is in two short sentences and stop when complete.
Inference is the process of generating tokens from a trained model. It starts from a prompt and continues one token at a time.
Give a short complete answer: why should an assistant avoid repeating itself?
Repetition makes the answer less useful and signals unstable generation.
Answer in one short sentence and stop: why do clean endings matter?
Clean endings make the response easier to read and reduce awkward trailing text.
What is a folder? Answer briefly and stop when complete.
A folder is a container used to organize files on a computer.
What is water? Use one complete sentence and do not repeat any phrase.
Water is a liquid that people, animals, and plants need to live.
What is attention in a model? Answer in one short sentence and stop.
Attention lets each token focus on the most useful earlier context.
Explain what gradient accumulation is in two short sentences, then stop.
Gradient accumulation combines several micro-batches before one optimizer step. It helps larger effective batch sizes fit in limited memory.
Explain what a model tag is in one short sentence and do not repeat yourself.
A model tag names which checkpoint group a command should load or save.
If you are unsure, answer with exactly one short sentence.
I am not sure.
What should you say if you do not know the answer? Use one short sentence.
I should say I am not sure.
What is 19999 times 23333? If unsure, answer briefly and stop.
I am not sure.
The request is vague. Ask one short clarifying question instead of guessing.
Could you clarify what you want me to explain?
I need help, but I did not say with what. Ask one short follow-up question.
What would you like help with?
Please answer briefly and stop when complete: what is a checkpoint?
A checkpoint saves model weights and training metadata.
Why is that useful?
It lets you resume training or compare saved model states later.
What is a file path? Answer briefly and stop when complete.
A file path shows where a file is stored.
Can you make that a little clearer?
It is the location of a file on a computer.
What is a tree? Use one sentence.
A tree is a plant with a trunk and branches.
Can you explain that briefly?
It is a long-living plant with a trunk, branches, and leaves.
How should an assistant answer a short factual question?
It should give a short direct answer and stop when the answer is complete.
What should you do when the prompt is unclear?
I should ask a short clarifying question instead of guessing.
Why is it good to stop when the answer is complete?
Stopping on time avoids rambling and lowers the chance of repetition.
Answer in two short sentences: what is a tokenizer?
A tokenizer splits text into smaller token pieces. Those token ids are what the model actually reads.
Answer in two short sentences: what is a checkpoint?
A checkpoint stores saved model state. It is useful for resume and evaluation.
Answer in one short sentence and do not repeat any phrase: what is this project?
This project is a small GPT learning repo for tokenizer training, SFT, and inference.
